{{Quote|I don't double deal on my employers, especially when working for the Klatooinian Trade Guild. You should know that.|Zo'Tannath apprehends Platt Okeefe|<ref name="PSG" />}}
'''Zo'Tannath''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who worked for the highest bidder.

==Biography==
Zo'Tannath kept most of her origins a mystery with few individuals ever seeing her face. She wore pieces of [[Ubese raider armor|Ubese armor]] scavenged from an [[Ubese/Legends|Ubese]] warrior she killed. Although proficient with a [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbine]] or [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], Zo'Tannath's preferred weapon was the [[Force pike/Legends|Force pike]], with which she could easily subdue most opponents.<ref name="PSG" />

Zo'Tannath picked up most of her bounties from [[Boztrok]] starport, which she frequented. She was merciless in carrying out her hunts, taking special pleasure in "dead-or-alive" bounties. This ensured her plenty of assignments from ruthless individuals from the fringe. She often made use of their [[Starship/Legends|starships]] when tracking her prey, and on occasion hired [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to aid her. She frequently used them to channel her prey into clever traps. Zo'Tannath never accepted any bargaining from her prey, remaining loyal to her employer despite offers of hefty bribes.<ref name="PSG" />

[[File:Dark_assassin.jpg|right|thumb|Zo'Tannath attacks Platt Okeefe]]
On one occasion, Zo'Tannath was hired by the [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanel]] [[Pok Nar-Ten]] to capture the smuggler [[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]], who had reneged on some ship payments. Zo'Tannath eventually tracked her down on the planet [[Wroona/Legends|Wroona]] and easily disarmed her. Zo'Tannath then took her into custody, giving Okeefe's [[Gruvian]] [[Gruvian Tovash|Tovash]] as a gift to the Nimbanel. Okeefe was later sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]].<ref name="PSG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Zo'Tannath's bio in ''Platt's Smugglers Guide'' does not identify her species, instead emphasizing her mysterious nature. Her Ubese armor is said to have been taken from one of her victims. However, the book also revealed Platt Okeefe's internal point of view, seeing Zo'Tannath as a human. This identification is complicated by the ''Ultimate Alien Anthology'', which lists the name Zo'Tannath as common among Ubese. This article follows Okeefe's identification, although there is some ambiguity in the matter.
